docs.title: RaBe Universal Base Image 10 Minimal
docs.text:
The RaBe Universal Base Image 10 Minimal is a stripped down image that uses microdnf for package management.

The image is based on the AlmaLinux 10 UBI10 variant image container provided by AlmaLinux and based on the work from Red Hat.

docs.title: Features
docs.text:
• Based on UBI10 minimal
  • Uses microdnf as a package manager
  • Establishes trust with the RaBe Root CA

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
Create a downstream image from ghcr.io/radiorabe/ubi10-minimal. Replace :latest with a specific version in the example below.

FROM ghcr.io/radiorabe/ubi10-minimal:latest

RUN <<-EOR
    set -xe
    microdnf install -y \
         shadow-utils
    microdnf clean all
    useradd -u 1001 -r -g 0 -s /sbin/nologin \
         -c "Default Application User" default
    microdnf remove -y \
         libsemanage \
         shadow-utils
EOR

USER 1001

Note that libsemanage is being removed because is was installed as a dependency of shadow-utils. We only need them for the useradd command so the safe solution is to remove both packages after use.

docs.title: Downstream Base Images
docs.text:
We provide specialised downstream images for select use cases.

  • TBD

docs.title: Advanced Usage
docs.text:
If you need packages from EPEL (like cowsay) your have to install an epel-release package first:

RUN    microdnf install -y epel-release \
    && microdnf install -y \
         cowsay \
    && microdnf clean all

To account for CIS-DI-0008 you may want to "defang" your image by running something similar to the following chmod after installing setuid/setgid binaries.

RUN    microdnf install -y \
         cowsay \
    && microdnf clean all \
    && chmod a-s \
         /usr/bin/* \
         /usr/sbin/* \
         /usr/libexec/*/*

docs.title: Release Management
docs.text:
The CI/CD setup uses semantic commit messages following the conventional commits standard. There is a GitHub Action in .github/workflows/semantic-release.yaml that uses go-semantic-commit to create new releases.

The commit message should be structured as follows:

<type>[optional scope]: <description>

[optional body]

[optional footer(s)]

The commit contains the following structural elements, to communicate intent to the consumers of your library:

  1. fix: a commit of the type fix patches gets released with a PATCH version bump
  2. feat: a commit of the type feat gets released as a MINOR version bump
  3. BREAKING CHANGE: a commit that has a footer BREAKING CHANGE: gets released as a MAJOR version bump
  4. types other than fix: and feat: are allowed and don't trigger a release

If a commit does not contain a conventional commit style message you can fix it during the squash and merge operation on the PR.

docs.title: Build Process
docs.text: The CI/CD setup uses the Docker build-push Action to publish container images. This is managed in .github/workflows/release.yaml.

docs.title: Purpose
docs.text:
This directory contains the MkDocs Material source for the documentation site published at radiorabe.github.io/container-image-ubi10-minimal.

The documentation explains how to use ghcr.io/radiorabe/ubi10-minimal as a base image, covers advanced usage patterns, and links to related monitoring resources in rabe-zabbix.

docs.title: Directory Layout
docs.text:
docs/
  gen_ref_pages.py    # Copies README.md → index.md at MkDocs build time (do not edit index.md directly)
  monitoring.md       # Monitoring guidance; references rabe-zabbix Zabbix templates
  css/
    style.css         # Custom theme overrides
  AGENTS.md           # This file

index.md is generated at build time — edit README.md in the repository root instead.

docs.title: Page Structure
docs.text:
Every documentation page must contain these sections in order:

  1. Title – # <Topic> and a one-sentence description of the page's scope.
  2. Body – Prose explanation, usage examples with fenced code blocks, and links to related resources.
  3. Links – Optional ## See Also section with links to related upstream docs.

docs.title: Key Conventions
docs.text:
• Single source of truth: User-facing content lives in README.md. The generator script copies it to index.md. Never edit index.md directly.
  • Code blocks: Use fenced code blocks with a language identifier. For Dockerfile snippets, use ```Dockerfile.
  • External links: Prefer linking to official upstream documentation rather than duplicating content.

docs.title: Navigation
docs.text: After adding a new page: 1. Register it in mkdocs.yml under the nav: key. 2. Add it to the llmstxt plugin sections: if it contains content useful for LLMs.

docs.title: Local Preview
docs.text:
pip install mkdocs mkdocs-material mkdocs-gen-files mkdocs-literate-nav mkdocs-section-index mkdocs-llmstxt
mkdocs serve

Open http://127.0.0.1:8000 to preview changes before committing.

docs.title: Monitoring
docs.text: Container images built on ghcr.io/radiorabe/ubi10-minimal can be monitored with Zabbix using the templates maintained in radiorabe/rabe-zabbix.

docs.title: Zabbix Templates
docs.text:
The rabe-zabbix repository provides OS-level and application-level Zabbix templates for Radio Bern RaBe infrastructure. Hosts running containers built on this image are typically assigned an OS stack template that covers base system health, and one or more application templates for the workloads running inside the container.

Relevant template categories:

  • Stacks: Operating systems – OS-level stack templates (CPU, memory, disk, network) assigned per host.
  • Templates: Applications – Per-application monitoring templates that complement the OS stack.

docs.title: See Also
docs.text:
• rabe-zabbix on GitHub – full template library
  • Zabbix documentation – upstream Zabbix docs
